repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 Theorem 1: exact local AIPW representation and Riesz norm identity

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML and Kennedy2023DRCATE contain the standard AIPW/DR cancellation skeleton; verified at https://academic.oup.com/ectj/article/21/1/C1/5056401 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.14497

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 concerns shrinking HTE report frontiers, not the fixed-x0 SA-CATE absolute-error rate rho_n

published_prior_art.cite: Kennedy2023DRCATE gives a model-free DR CATE error decomposition and KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE gives Holder pointwise CATE rates; neither states this exact black-box local rho_n package. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.14497 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.00837

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier_v1 asks for DML/Riesz frontier channels, not a three-channel pointwise CATE Le Cam converse with b_n, p_n, and nuisance-product terms

published_prior_art.cite: JinSyrgkanis2025GeneralLinearFunctionalLowerBounds is closest and covers general structure-agnostic linear-functional lower bounds, but the verified abstract/examples do not state the localized pointwise CATE converse with b_n and p_n. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.17341

structure_flags.label: Section 4 / Section 8
structure_flags.one_line: The drafter handoff lists Theorem 1, but the proposal demotes it to an unnumbered supporting identity and Section 4 P4 lists only Conjectures 1-2.

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The bibkey BenkeserCaiVanDerLaan2024SelectiveDR is attached to the Biometrika 111(2):517-535 selective-machine-learning paper, whose verified authors are Cui and Tchetgen Tchetgen, not Benkeser, Cai, and van der Laan.

novelty_flags.label: BenkeserCaiVanDerLaan2024SelectiveDR
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal and checklist misidentify the selective machine-learning comparator; the verified Biometrika paper is 'Selective machine learning of doubly robust functionals' by Cui and Tchetgen Tchetgen, not a 2024 Benkeser-Cai-van der Laan paper.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: triangular-array empirical-process control for cross-fit localized DR scores is conjectural
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: upper-rate claim is conditional on proving local oracle-score concentration and foldwise product-drift control under Assumptions 1-5

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Assumption local-rich supplies perturbability for the three Le Cam channels but does not construct the pairs
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: matching-converse claim is field-tier and conditional on making the local bias, sampling, and nuisance-product perturbations feasible

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Either restore the localized AIPW identity as a numbered Theorem 1 with a matching Section 4 P4 entry, or remove Theorem 1 from the handoff and per-result inventory.
Replace BenkeserCaiVanDerLaan2024SelectiveDR with the correct CuiTchetgenTchetgen2024SelectiveDR citation, or drop it if it is only adjacent background.

Add theorem- or section-level anchors for JinSyrgkanis2025GeneralLinearFunctionalLowerBounds explaining why its general functional lower bound does not already subsume the localized CATE point functional.
Add one small worked Le Cam perturbation for the nuisance-product channel so Assumption local-rich is less promissory before Stage 0.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the field-tier kernel survives, but the result inventory and misidentified Biometrika comparator need a local patch before Stage 0.
